sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(2) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland streams, rivers, and canals)
sources.quotedText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 267239, "Sherwin Creek", Stream, CA, Mono Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":267239,"gaz_name":"Sherwin Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Mono","objectid_1":1404119}. Corrects the file's prior lon/lat (-118.9432, 37.6094, ~2.6 km southwest of the GNIS point, source unclear) to the GNIS-recorded coordinate (-118.9376, 37.6324), which lands within ~350 m of Sherwin Creek Campground (37.629935, -118.9359658 per HomeTownLocator) near the creek's upper course east of Mammoth Lakes.

parameters.note: Sherwin Creek (Mono Co.) is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide regulation for inland streams, rivers, and canals in §5.85(a)(2).

regulations.humanSummary: Open with no gear restrictions from the last Saturday in April through November 15; open catch-and-release for trout (artificial lures with barbless hooks only) from November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April (general statewide stream regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland streams, rivers, and canals, except those listed in Sections 7.40(b) and 7.50(b), are open to fishing from the last Saturday in April through November 15 with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit, with no gear restrictions. From November 16 through the Friday preceding the last Saturday in April, a zero-trout bag limit applies, and only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used for all species of fish. In waters where the bag limit for trout is zero, trout must be released unharmed and should not be removed from the water.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland streams, rivers, and canals during the take season (last Saturday in April through November 15): five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Sherwin Creek (Region 6, Mono Co.) for catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Take program, with a stated release month range of April-November. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
